text: WHAT LAY BELOW The Mall was built over an abandoned mine. The opening created in the bunker connects the basement to the once dormant mining tunnels. ▶The tunnels are dark, with light sources being required. The walls are hewn from stone and dirt. ▶Old wood reinforces the pathways. ▶A duffle bag containing zip ties, rope, and a Polaroid camera is stashed in a small alcove here. ▶The passageways vary in size and skill in construction, weaving alongside natural formations. Paths branching off into darkness. Three pathways connect to: ▶B. Tunnel of Teeth ▶C. Replaced ▶E. The Tangle (p. 24) A. ENTRY TO THE MINES The path turns to reveal a tight passage lined with jagged teeth [Save or d4 damage]. Three pathways connect to: ▶A. Entry to the Mines ▶E. The Tangle (p. 24) ▶G. Movement in the Darkness (p. 24) B. TUNNEL OF TEETH The sound of persistent wet squishing echos prior to their arrival. The tunnel is completely covered in eyes. They vary in color and shape. All look at those who approach. Three pathways connect to: ▶C. Replaced ▶E. The Tangle (p. 24) ▶H. Ventilation Access (p. 24) D. TUNNEL OF EYES In the far reaches of the sub- basements hang a massive reinforced set of double doors. ▶A padlock clasps these doors shut. ▶Keys are found: �In Billy’s Office(p. 14). �On Mr. Neil’s (p. 6). �The lock can be broken with tools or force, but failing can cause bodily harm, break the tool or alert others to the party’s presence. ▶Inside is an underground shelter with everything a small group (up to 10) would need to survive. ▶The bunks against the back wall have been torn out and cast aside. ▶A crack in the wall has been widened into a large opening, revealing a tunnel leading into the damp, dark earth. ▶Cans of gasoline, two headlamps, a pickax lay beside the opening. 24. THE BUNKER A REPLACED NPC (p. 8) lays here, unconscious. If awoken they will ask the party for help. Three pathways connect to: ▶A. Entry to the Mines ▶D. Tunnel of Eyes ▶E. The Tangle (p. 24) C. REPLACED [24] The Mines // The Tangle is the central hub down here. F. MEMORY GARDEN The tunnel branches off, narrowing and rising until it is a vertical shaft. An old ragged rope and piton are set into its side, allowing access to a vent grate and the mall’s ventilation system (p. 13). Four pathways connect to: ▶4. The Vents (p. 13) ▶D. Tunnel of Eyes (p. 23) ▶E. The Tangle ▶L. Taken (p. 25) H. VENTILATION ACCESS A mess of weaving passageways and tunnels, often turning around and leading back on themselves. A hub in which it is easy to get lost within. Nine pathways of note connect to The Tangle: ▶A. Entry to the Mines (p. 23) ▶B. Tunnel of Teeth (p. 23) ▶C. Replaced (p. 23) ▶D. Tunnel of Eyes (p. 23) ▶G. Movement in the Darkness ▶H. Ventilation Access ▶I. Fruiting Body ▶J. Child of Ammon ▶L. Taken (p. 25) E. THE TANGLE The party is being hunted through the tunnels. Successful DEX keeps them safe, but any failure will result in taking damage, losing an item or becoming separated. Three pathways connect to: ▶B. Tunnel of Teeth (p. 23) ▶E. The Tangle ▶F. The Memory Garden G. MOVEMENT IN THE DARKNESS The space bends and changes, reenacting a warped memory made of flesh. ▶The space attempts to isolate the party and keep them here. ▶A randomly chosen player gets a Whisper Card (p. 37), as a pivotal moment from their PC’s past plays out around them, in a warped, funhouse style. Two pathways connect to: ▶G. Movement in the Darkness ▶N. The Portal (p. 27) Stagnant spores around a Fruiting Body (p. 13). ▶The leading party member - Whisper Card (p. 37). ▶Others must make save/plan to avoid the spores or get a Whisper Card (p. 37). Four pathways connect to: ▶F. The Memory Garden ▶G. Movement in the Darkness ▶E. The Tangle ▶M. The Fungal Fields (p. 26) I. FRUITING BODY Mid-transformation, part of an NPC the party has met previously is visible in the mass of flesh. Three pathways connect to: ▶E. The Tangle ▶K. Mine Tunnel (p. 25) ▶M. The Fungal Fields (p. 26) J. CHILD OF AMMON The Mines // Being separated can lead to fate far worse than death. [25] A branching path. To the right, a faint shadow of a person beckons the PCs. Each PC must make a CTRL save. ▶ Success: The shadow doesn’t look or sound right (1 Stress). ▶ Failure: The person they love most beckons them down the right path - Whisper Card (p. 37): �Characters who follow reach a dead end with a narrow Hole in the floor. �Eyes peer out, as the “person” begs them to enter. Entering the Hole: The first PC that enters crawls a ways until they come to a tight bend. A hand reaches out to them. ▶ If they take it: They crawl onward, through darkness and lose communication with the party. They will reappear later, in the Fungal Fields (p. 26). �They have been REPLACED. Hand them the blank Whisper Card. ▶ If they refuse: Reveal it was actually a dead end. Allow them to crawl out. ▶Additional players who enter reach an impassable choke point and need help exiting - Whisper Card (p. 37). L. TAKEN An exposed vein above a stash of old mining equipment. Includes an expired box of TNT. Four pathways connect to: ▶J. Child of Ammon (p. 24) ▶L. Taken (p. 25) ▶M. The Fungal Fields (p. 26) ▶N. The Portal (p. 27) K. MINE TUNNEL SEPARATED Navigating dark spaces always carries the risk of getting separated. Use these rules for being separated or escaping the mines. ▶Being separated triggers a Whisper Card (p. 37). ▶Pass a CTRL save to be reunited, if you are separated. ▶Failure can result in: �Being attacked. �Finding an item from
